MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2018 Continued To Apr-13-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT/CROSS DEFENDANT AARON BROTHERS, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS- COMPLAINANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT.

Defendant Aaron Brothers, Inc.'s motion to strike portions of the first amended cross-complaint is continued to April 13, 2018 to give the parties an opportunity to meet and confer and enter into a stipulation to allow the filing of a second amended cross-complaint. The court adopts the request made in the letter of March 8, 2018 from Mr. Shi.

Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-06-2017 Continued To Feb-21-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT/CROSS-DEFENDANT JOHN PANOS & JOHN PANOS AS TRUSTEE'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant John Panos' motion to strike portions of the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Ellen Cevarich is denied as to Ms. Cevarich's punitive damages allegation and granted without leave to amend as to count 2 for failure to warn in the first cause of action for premises liability. Ms. Cevarich's allegations that CCSF warned Mr. Panos and the Trust of the dangerous sidewalk and they failed to remedy the sidewalk or warn of the danger are sufficient to support the punitive damages allegations. Civil Code 846 is inapposite because there are no allegations indicating that Ms. Cevarich was using the property for a "recreational purpose" within the meaning of the statute.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185, email:ADelgado@sftc.org, Reported. (D302)
